frame_analyses.description: Initial setup with needle driver and grasper positioned. Purple suture already placed through foam pad with needled end visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture appears pre-placed, needle driver approaching for loading

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver grasping needle. Suture positioned across foam pad in curved pattern.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp with driver, suture has appropriate curve across tissue

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot construction. Suture being manipulated to form first throw configuration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initial throw formation in progress, instruments working together

frame_analyses.description: First throw being tightened down. Suture forming initial knot configuration.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw appears to be a surgeon's knot with double wrap, good tension

frame_analyses.description: Second throw construction. Instruments manipulating suture for opposite-direction throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear hand alternation for second throw, proper knot construction technique

frame_analyses.description: Second throw being secured. Knot taking shape with good tissue approximation.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good tightening technique, knot appears secure and well-formed

frame_analyses.description: Third throw construction and tightening. Final security throw being completed.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Additional security throw being added, proper hand alternation maintained

frame_analyses.description: Final knot complete with good tissue approximation. No cutting visible in this clip.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Secure final knot with excellent tissue approximation, no visible gaps

knot_assessments.notes: Double-wrapped surgeon's knot for initial security
Proper hand alternation, single throw opposite direction
Final security throw, excellent final configuration

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Excellent tissue approximation with no visible gaps, suture appears to close simulated incision completely

technique_summary: Excellent intracorporeal knot tying technique with proper surgeon's knot initiation, consistent hand alternation, and outstanding final tissue approximation. Very efficient completion in 21 seconds.
improvement_suggestions: Consider adding one more security throw for maximum knot security
Practice suture cutting to complete the full task sequence
strengths: Excellent tissue approximation with no gaps
Very efficient timing and smooth technique
Proper use of surgeon's knot for initial security
